original_reasoning: Arabic gloss explicitly preserves a proper name, and Greek is likewise a preserved proper name form.
Arabic deep sleep or cessation strongly matches Sabbath as rest or stoppage.
تيريز الثوب والأرض points to patterned inlay or banding, close to interwoven with gold.
الحرث is tillage/ploughing, and ἕλκηθρον is the stock of the plough; this is a strong action-to-tool match.
The Arabic gloss directly centers on subsiding, calming, and becoming still, which strongly matches ἡσυχάζετε, be quiet or at rest.
Arabic explicitly gives a fortress or place-name, and Greek is likewise the place-name Sybrita; this is a strong toponymic match.
βαρακίνη refers to a kind of garment/cloth, which aligns closely with the Arabic gloss 'a black cloak'.
Arabic الشاطر here carries rogue or outlaw agency, which matches Greek robber very closely.
